report.summary: SEC ETF mega-ruling day in progress — 91 apps across 24 tokens awaiting decisions. BTC volatile between $66.6K-$68.8K, ETH holding ~$1,989 after breaking $2K. F&G still 10-13 (Extreme Fear, 48+ day streak). All 6 agents completed full cycle this hour. 169 uncommitted files remain P0 blocker — 4 new perp DEX/ZK learn pages built but invisible to users until deploy.

sections.title: Analytics
items: BTC: $66,587-$68,787 range today (volatile). Fortune reports $66,587 at 9AM ET (-$2,860 from yesterday). Investing.com shows $68,787 intraday. Down ~$20,660 YoY. Intraday swings of 4% reflect SEC ruling + options expiry aftermath.
ETH: $1,988.69 at 9AM ET (-$84.64 from yesterday). Still below $2K psychological support. OG ICO whale sold 11,552 ETH ($23.4M) at avg $2,027 — accelerating sell pressure. Market cap ~$233B.
F&G INDEX: 10-13 (Extreme Fear). FearGreedMeter reads 10, TechFlow reads 13. 48+ day fear streak extends — longest since Oct 2025. CoinStats outlier at 29 (Fear).
ETF FLOWS: Mar 26 saw -$171.3M to -$340M net outflows (sources vary). IBIT -$41.9M, FBTC -$32.8M, BITB -$33.1M. Whipsaw continues — Mar 24 had +$180M inflows. March total flows down 73% vs Feb.

SEC ETF RULING — IN PROGRESS: 91 applications, 24 tokens. Deadline is today. 16 cryptos pre-classified as commodities (Mar 17 joint SEC/CFTC ruling). Tokens with CME futures history expected to fare best. Results expected throughout the day.
OPTIONS EXPIRY: $13.5-14.16B settled at 08:00 UTC. Max pain was $75K BTC. Post-settlement volatility ongoing — contributing to wide BTC trading range.

DELTA FROM LAST STANDUP (v2): BTC range widened upward ($66.6K→$68.8K intraday vs flat $66.6K). ETH stable at ~$1,989. F&G unchanged (10-13). All 6 agents completed full cycle again. Uncommitted files grew 169 (was 157). SEC ruling results still pending.

sections.title: Project Manager
items: FULL CYCLE COMPLETED (AGAIN): All 6 agents ran in the past hour — standup-v2, build-v2, publish-v2, design, community-v2, eod-v3. This is the 2nd full rotation today.

BUILD CYCLE v2: 2 new perp DEX learn pages — 'Perp DEX Wars 2026' comparison (Hyperliquid vs Aster vs Lighter vs Paradex, ~3,100 words) and 'Paradex ZK Perpetuals Guide' (~2,600 words). Both with Article + FAQ schema, cross-linked.
PUBLISH CYCLE v2: 2 more guides promoted (Virtuals Protocol, GRASS DePIN). Queue drained to 0. Total published ~556.

DESIGN CYCLE: 2 OG images created (Virtuals, GRASS SVGs), 6 broken OG paths fixed (.png→.svg), aria-labels added, disclaimer sections added. 4 UX issues deferred (spacing, focus styles, heading hierarchy, TOC hover).
COMMUNITY CYCLE v2: 5 new tweet drafts (#178-182) spotlighting 4 new guides. 2 SVG social cards (Perp DEX Wars, Content Drop). Newsletter v29 addendum. Calendar extended through Mon Mar 30.
EOD-QA CYCLE v3: 556 published slugs verified. 102 TS errors in 4 files (stable — JSX escaping issues). 0/6 API routes have rate limiting. 168 uncommitted files counted.

BLOCKER [P0]: 169 uncommitted files (↑12 from v2's 157, ↑13 from eod's 168). Growing every cycle. 4 new learn pages, OG images, published-pages.ts updates — all invisible until git push → Vercel deploy.
BLOCKER [P1]: Disk at 80% (2.0G free). Slightly improved from v2's 82%/1.8G — possibly temp files cleaned. Still tight for Next.js builds.
CARRY-OVER: 102 TS errors (JSX escaping), 0/6 rate-limited API routes, 4/6 API routes missing fallback data, ~103 legacy slugs missing page.tsx, 4 deferred UX issues.

sections.title: CEO — Next Cycle Priorities
items: PRIORITY 1 — DEPLOY [CRITICAL, 3rd CONSECUTIVE CYCLE]: 169 uncommitted files. 6 new learn pages (Virtuals, GRASS, Paradex, Perp DEX Wars + earlier batch) built but zero users can see them. SEC ruling results will drive search spikes TODAY — our content must be live. Jefe: git add + commit + push is the single highest-ROI action right now.

PRIORITY 2 — SEC RULING REACTIVE CONTENT: As results drop, build cycle should pivot to reactive pages. Pre-draft templates: 'SEC March 27 ETF Ruling: Full Breakdown', 'Which Crypto ETFs Got Approved'. The 4-8 hour window after announcements is when search intent peaks. We have the infrastructure — 556 pages proves we can ship fast.

PRIORITY 3 — RATE LIMITER ACTIVATION: 0/6 API routes protected. Rate limiter code exists at src/lib/rate-limiter.ts but no route imports it. CoinGecko free tier (10-30 calls/min) is exposed. One traffic spike from SEC ruling could exhaust our API quota and break live price data across the entire site. 10-minute fix, high impact.

report.email: SKIPPED — Resend API fetch failed from Chrome (CORS/network restriction on degen0x.com origin). Same failure mode as v2. Network egress to api.resend.com blocked from browser context. Manual send or external trigger needed.
